source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane Incident, VR Block 38M

event.top_event: Casing lifting pin failure striking worker
actions_taken: Injured person evaluated and prepared for movement
Personnel basket used to lift injured person to liftboat
Injured person transported to Lafayette General Hospital at 6:00 p.m.
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on July 14, 2025
Independent third-party pull testing conducted on July 23, 2025
Crane main hoist line replaced on July 20, 2025
Casing jacks used to remove stuck casing on July 29, 2025
event.summary: On July 12, 2025, at approximately 3:30 p.m., a casing lifting pin failed during well abandonment operations on the Vermilion Block 38M platform operated by Chevron U.S.A. Inc. The failed pin, which was bent into a U-shape, struck a Supreme Services employee in the face, causing a subdural hematoma, blunt trauma, an open fracture of the right side of the base of the skull, and multiple closed fractures of facial bones. The incident resulted in a required evacuation via medevac helicopter. Root causes included: (1) installation of a spool riser with an 11.00-inch inside diameter above a seal ring with an 11.44-inch outside diameter, causing the casing to become stuck; (2) the crane operator exceeding the safe working load limit of the casing lifting pin (60,000 pounds) while attempting to lift the stuck casing with the main hoist; and (3) incorrect installation of the two-part sling with D-rings not placed directly adjacent to the casing. Independent testing demonstrated that when D-rings were positioned 3-4 inches from the casing (as they were during the incident), the pin failed at 102,500 pounds, compared to successful performance at 150,000 pounds when D-rings were properly positioned adjacent to the casing.
recommendations: BSEE Lake Charles District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations issue a Safety Alert/Bulletin regarding this incident
key_phrases: seal ring stuck in spool riser

threats.name: Casing becomes stuck in wellhead due to seal ring diameter exceeding spool riser diameter
Crane operator applies excessive lifting force to unstuck casing
Casing lifting pin subjected to bending loads due to improper sling installation
Lifting pin exceeds safe working load limit

consequences.name: Casing lifting pin failure and projectile strike to worker
Worker injury - subdural hematoma and facial fractures
Damage to crane main hoist wire rope

controls.name: Chevron Crane Lifting Plan
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to establish safe lifting procedures and weight limits
controls.lod_basis: Approved lifting plan with maximum weight capacity of 24,255 pounds

supporting_text: According to the Chevron lift plan that was partially completed for the initial casing pull, the Max Weight Capacity to be pulled could not exceed 24,255 pounds.
The crane operator's statement indicates that the first attempted lift of the 7-inch casing was using the auxiliary hoist, instead of the main hoist as planned.

the crane operator's statement noted, 'Casing pipe got stuck in hole while using the AUX line block! So, I was ask to bring down big blk to hook up slings and pull casing pipe up,' which would indicate that the Max Weight Capacity for this pull was likely over-pulled with the use of a larger capacity main hoist.

controls.name: Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) for crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Completed and signed by Helix personnel prior to lifting operations

supporting_text: During the meeting, Helix personnel completed and signed a Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) for the crane operations.

The Helix crane operations JSEA and Supreme's pulling casing with crane BJSEA did not mention the maximum lifting weight capacity of 24,255 pounds, which was calculated in Chevron's approved lifting plan.

controls.name: Behavioral Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (BJSEA) for crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify behavioral hazards and safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Completed and signed by Supreme personnel prior to lifting operations

supporting_text: Separately, Supreme personnel completed and signed a Behavioral Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (BJSEA) for crane operations.

The Helix crane operations JSEA and Supreme's pulling casing with crane BJSEA did not mention the maximum lifting weight capacity of 24,255 pounds, which was calculated in Chevron's approved lifting plan.

controls.name: Ultimate Work Authority (UWA) review and approval of lifting plan
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify lifting plan safety and completeness
controls.lod_basis: UWA reviewed and approved the Chevron Crane Lifting Plan

supporting_text: Finally, the Ultimate Work Authority (UWA) reviewed and approved the Chevron Crane Lifting Plan for pulling the non-cemented production tubing and casings.

The plan was approved by the UWA on July 12, 2025, using the main hoist line, at 12:00 p.m. The investigators found the following issues with the plan: 1) the field for the name of the crane operator was left blank; 2) the crane operator was listed as the supervisor of the lift; 3) the crane's make/model was left blank; 4) the crane capacity at a boom angle of 65 degrees with a radius of 57.2 feet was calculated on the plan as 110,000 pounds while the load chart on the crane indicates approximately 179,102 pounds for a 65-degree angle at a radius of 57.2 feet; and 5) the max capacity of the crane on the plan is listed incorrectly as 9,900 pounds instead of 99,000 pounds.

controls.name: Wellhead component dimensional verification
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure compatibility of wellhead components
controls.lod_basis: Verification that spool riser inside diameter matches seal ring outside diameter

supporting_text: Supreme and NNW workers removed part of the wellhead exposing the seal ring sitting on top of the casing hanger. They installed a spool riser onto the wellhead, which had an inside diameter of 11.00 inches, on top of the exposed seal ring, which had a diameter of 11.44 inches.
A spool riser, which had an inside diameter of 11.00 inches, was installed onto the wellhead on top of the exposed seal ring, which had a diameter of 11.44 inches.

the operator installed a spool riser with an inside diameter of 11.00 inches above a wellhead seal ring with an outside diameter of 11.44 inches. This dimensional incompatibility caused the seal ring to become stuck inside the spool riser during an attempted lift and retrieval of the casing.

controls.name: Casing lifting pin installation procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper installation of lifting pin and sling
controls.lod_basis: NNW procedure NNW-PRO-SOP.140 and certified pin pull analysis requiring D-rings adjacent to casing

supporting_text: In the certified pull pin analysis, there is a note that states, 'the pull linkages (D-rings) are required to be adjacent to the caisson in order to minimize bending load on the pin.' The pictures taken by OII at the NNW yard at the NNW shop in Carrier, Mississippi, show markings on the pin indicating the D-rings were not adjacent to the casing. It was estimated that the D-rings were approximately two inches from the casing.
The two-part sling was not installed correctly onto the casing lifting pin. The D-rings were not placed directly adjacent to the casing.
The pictures taken by OII at the NNW yard at the NNW shop in Carrier, Mississippi, show markings on the pin indicating the D-rings were not adjacent to the casing.

controls.name: NNW procedure for casing cut and retrieve
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to provide safe work procedures for casing operations
controls.lod_basis: NNW-PRO-SOP.140 Single String Casing Cut and Retrieve procedure

supporting_text: The LCD investigator's review of NNW procedure used for this job, 'NNW-PRO-SOP.140 Single String Casing Cut and Retrieve,' was written for a drilling rig utilizing a Top Drive with Bails, not a crane on a liftboat.

Furthermore, the procedure incorrectly states that the lifting capacity for 2-inch casing pins is 67,000 pounds; whereas, a certified two-inch pin pull analysis dated October 25, 2019, provided by NNW, states the safe working load limit is 60,000 pounds.

controls.name: Crane load monitoring and alarm system
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to detect and prevent overload conditions
controls.lod_basis: Onboard weight display with audible alarms and lockout at maximum lifting point

supporting_text: The LCD investigators were informed by the crane operator that the auxiliary hoist and main hoist lines are monitored by an onboard display. The crane operator explained that both hoists have audible alarms that sound when reaching the maximum lifting point, which is determined by the crane's radius and boom angle. When the maximum weight limit is reached, the hoists are locked out from lifting a heavier weight.
The LCD investigators were also informed the weight indicator had an approximate one second delay from the sensor to the onboard display, which shows the current lifting weight.

controls.name: All-stop procedure for stuck casing
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt operations and reassess when casing becomes stuck
controls.lod_basis: Standard procedure to call all-stop and review plan when unexpected conditions occur

supporting_text: An all-stop was also not called to address the stuck casing.
The crane lifted the casing until it became stuck in the well; they then set the slips and cut 17.77 feet of casing from this first section.

controls.name: Crane inspection and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure crane equipment is in safe operating condition
controls.lod_basis: Inspection of crane following shock load incident and replacement of damaged components

supporting_text: A review of the crane documents found that on July 13, 2025, the liftboat Dallas's crane was inspected due to being shock loaded during the incident. The crane technician inspected the main hoist line and stated that he 'found 1 strand damaged and 3 other strands with damaged wires.' The main hoist line was put out of service.

on July 20, 2025, an S&A Services crane technician at the Helix yard in Fourchon, Louisiana, replaced the damaged main hoist line on the liftboat Dallas's crane. The technician also completed a quarterly inspection, subsequently returning the main hoist to service.

controls.name: Emergency response and medical evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative and engineering control to minimize injury severity and provide rapid medical care
controls.lod_basis: Immediate medical response, medevac helicopter coordination, and transport to hospital

supporting_text: The medic was called to scene immediately following the incident. The medic evaluated the IP and prepared him to be moved. The IP was then placed in a personnel basket to be lifted from the platform to the liftboat. A medevac helicopter was called at 3:36 p.m. While waiting on the helicopter, the IP was moved to the liftboat TV room for observation and first aid. The medevac helicopter landed on the liftboat at 5:45 p.m. and departed with the IP for Lafayette General Hospital at 6:00 p.m.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
